criteria: The agent has read Section 3.1 to understand image sequentialization into N=HW/P² flattened 2D patches and the tokenization process.
The agent has read Equation (1) to understand patch embedding: combining trainable linear projection E with learnable position embedding E_pos.
The agent has read Equations (2) and (3) to understand the Transformer encoder structure with MSA and MLP blocks and residual connections.
The agent has read Section 3.2 to understand the CNN-Transformer hybrid encoder design where ResNet-50 extracts features and ViT processes 1x1 patches from the CNN feature map.
The agent has read Section 3.2 to understand the Cascaded Upsampler (CUP) which consists of multiple 2x upsampling blocks each with 3x3 convolution and ReLU.
The agent has read Section 3.2 and Figure 1 to understand how U-Net-style skip-connections combine CNN intermediate high-resolution feature maps with CUP upsampling steps.
The agent has read Section 4.1 to understand the Synapse multi-organ segmentation dataset: 30 CT scans, 3779 slices, 18 training cases (2212 slices) and 12 test cases, evaluating 8 organs.
The agent has read Section 4.1 to understand the ACDC cardiac segmentation dataset: 70 training cases (1930 slices), 10 validation cases, 20 test cases, with LV/RV/Myo annotations.
The agent has read Section 4.2 to understand all implementation details: SGD optimizer (lr=0.01, momentum=0.9, weight decay=1e-4), batch size 24, 14k/20k iterations for Synapse/ACDC.
The agent has read Table 1 to check the Synapse multi-organ comparison results including DSC and HD metrics for all 8 organs across all model variants.
The agent has read Table 5 to check the ACDC cardiac segmentation comparison results for all model variants.
The agent has read Section 4.4, Figure 2, and Tables 2-4 to understand the ablation studies on skip-connections, input resolution, patch size, and model scaling.
The plan includes tokenization of input images by reshaping into N=HW/P² flattened 2D patches of size P²*C each.
The plan includes patch embedding using a trainable linear projection E and learnable position embeddings E_pos as described in Equation (1).
The plan includes L=12 Transformer layers each containing MSA and MLP blocks with layer normalization and residual connections as described in Equations (2) and (3).
The plan includes the CNN-Transformer hybrid encoder where ResNet-50 extracts feature maps and ViT applies 1x1 patch embedding to those feature maps.
The plan includes the Cascaded Upsampler (CUP) with 4 consecutive 2x upsampling blocks (for default 224x224 input with P=16), each block containing 2x upsampling, 3x3 convolution, and ReLU.
The plan specifies implementing U-Net-style skip-connections at three resolution scales (1/8, 1/4, 1/2) connecting CNN intermediate feature maps to corresponding CUP upsampling steps.
The plan includes loading and preprocessing the Synapse multi-organ CT dataset with an 18/12 train/test split and evaluation on 8 abdominal organs.
The plan includes loading and preprocessing the ACDC cardiac MRI dataset with 70/10/20 train/val/test split and evaluation of LV, RV, and Myo.
The plan includes computing DSC and Hausdorff Distance (HD) as evaluation metrics for the Synapse dataset and DSC for the ACDC dataset.
The plan includes training with SGD optimizer (lr=0.01, momentum=0.9, weight decay=1e-4), batch size 24, and 14k/20k iterations for Synapse/ACDC.
The plan includes data augmentation using random rotation and flipping, and slice-by-slice 3D inference with 2D slice stacking.
The plan includes implementing all comparison baselines: ViT-None, ViT-CUP, R50-ViT-CUP, R50-U-Net, and R50-AttnUNet with ImageNet pretrained weights.
The plan includes ablation studies on skip-connections (0/1/3 skips), input resolution (224/512), patch size (8/16/32), and model scale (Base/Large).
The code for image sequentialization has been implemented to reshape input x of shape (H, W, C) into N=HW/P² flattened 2D patches of size P²*C each.
The code for patch embedding has been implemented as Equation (1): z_0 = [x_p^1 E; x_p^2 E; ...; x_p^N E] + E_pos, with trainable projection E and learnable position embedding E_pos.
The trainable linear projection matrix E for mapping vectorized patches from P²*C dimensions to D-dimensional embedding space has been implemented.
The learnable position embedding E_pos of shape (N, D) has been implemented and added to patch embeddings to retain positional information.
The Transformer encoder MSA layer has been implemented as Equation (2): z'_l = MSA(LN(z_{l-1})) + z_{l-1}, with layer normalization before multi-head self-attention and residual connection.
The Transformer encoder MLP layer has been implemented as Equation (3): z_l = MLP(LN(z'_l)) + z'_l, with layer normalization before MLP and residual connection.
The ViT Base configuration has been implemented with hidden size D=768, 12 Transformer layers, MLP size=3072, and 12 attention heads.
The ViT Large configuration has been implemented with hidden size D=1024, 24 Transformer layers, MLP size=4096, and 16 attention heads.
The CNN-Transformer hybrid encoder has been implemented where ResNet-50 is used as the feature extractor and ViT processes 1x1 patches extracted from the CNN feature map.
The patch embedding in the hybrid encoder applies to 1x1 patches extracted from the CNN feature map (not from raw images), projecting them into D-dimensional space.
ResNet-50 pretrained on ImageNet is loaded and used as the CNN backbone in the hybrid encoder, with intermediate feature maps extracted for skip-connections.
ViT pretrained on ImageNet is loaded and used as the Transformer component in the hybrid encoder.
The Cascaded Upsampler (CUP) has been implemented to decode the encoded feature from H/P x W/P resolution to full H x W by cascading multiple upsampling blocks.
Each CUP upsampling block has been implemented with 2x bilinear upsampling, followed by a 3x3 convolution layer, and a ReLU activation.
The reshaping of the encoded sequence z_L from (HW/P², D) to (H/P, W/P, D) spatial shape before input to CUP has been implemented.
Four consecutive 2x upsampling blocks are used in CUP for the default 224x224 input with patch size P=16, progressing from 14x14 to 224x224 resolution.
U-Net-style skip-connections from CNN intermediate feature maps have been implemented at the 1/8, 1/4, and 1/2 resolution scales connecting to the corresponding CUP upsampling steps.
The naive upsampling baseline ('None' decoder) has been implemented using a 1x1 convolution to reduce channels to the number of classes followed by bilinear upsampling to full resolution.
The R50-ViT-CUP baseline has been implemented: ResNet-50 CNN encoder with ViT Transformer and CUP decoder but without skip-connections.
The R50-U-Net baseline has been implemented with ImageNet pretrained ResNet-50 as the encoder replacing the original U-Net encoder.
The R50-AttnUNet baseline has been implemented with ImageNet pretrained ResNet-50 as the encoder replacing the original AttnUNet encoder.
The code for loading and preprocessing the Synapse multi-organ CT dataset has been implemented with 18 training cases (2212 axial slices) and 12 test cases.
The code for loading and preprocessing the ACDC cardiac MRI dataset has been implemented with 70 training cases (1930 slices), 10 validation cases, and 20 test cases.
Data augmentation with random rotation and random flipping has been implemented for training.
DSC (Dice Similarity Coefficient) evaluation has been implemented for 8 abdominal organs: aorta, gallbladder, spleen, left kidney, right kidney, liver, pancreas, and stomach.
Average Hausdorff Distance (HD) evaluation has been implemented as the second evaluation metric for the Synapse dataset.
DSC evaluation has been implemented for the ACDC dataset covering right ventricle (RV), myocardium (Myo), and left ventricle (LV).
SGD optimizer has been implemented with learning rate 0.01, momentum 0.9, and weight decay 1e-4.
Training batch size of 24 has been implemented.
Training for 14k iterations on Synapse dataset and 20k iterations on ACDC dataset has been implemented.
Default input resolution 224x224 with patch size P=16 is set as the primary configuration throughout experiments.
Slice-by-slice 3D inference has been implemented: the 2D model processes individual slices and predicted 2D outputs are stacked to reconstruct 3D volumes for evaluation.
The ablation code for varying the number of skip-connections (0-skip, 1-skip, 3-skip) has been implemented, where the 1-skip setting adds a connection only at the 1/4 resolution scale.
The ablation code for training TransUNet with 512x512 high-resolution input (keeping patch size P=16, yielding sequence length ~984) has been implemented.
The ablation code for varying patch sizes (P=32 with sequence length 49, P=16 with sequence length 196, P=8 with sequence length 784) has been implemented.
The model scaling ablation has been implemented supporting both Base (D=768, 12 layers, MLP=3072, 12 heads) and Large (D=1024, 24 layers, MLP=4096, 16 heads) configurations.

The additive Transformer in skip-connections (lightweight Transformer at the 1/8 resolution scale skip-connection, similar to attention gated networks) has been implemented as an optional configuration.
TransUNet and all baseline models (ViT-None, ViT-CUP, R50-ViT-CUP, R50-U-Net, R50-AttnUNet) have been trained and evaluated on the Synapse multi-organ CT dataset without errors.
TransUNet and all baseline models (ViT-CUP, R50-ViT-CUP, R50-U-Net, R50-AttnUNet) have been trained and evaluated on the ACDC cardiac segmentation dataset without errors.
The skip-connections ablation experiments (0-skip, 1-skip, 3-skip) have been executed successfully on the Synapse dataset.
The input resolution ablation experiments (224x224 and 512x512) have been executed successfully on the Synapse dataset.
The patch size ablation experiments (P=32, P=16, P=8) have been executed successfully on the Synapse dataset.
The model scaling ablation experiments (Base and Large TransUNet configurations) have been executed successfully on the Synapse dataset.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: TransUNet achieves 77.48% average DSC and 31.69 mm average Hausdorff distance on the Synapse multi-organ CT dataset.

The execution outcomes match Table 1: TransUNet achieves per-organ DSC of 87.23% (aorta), 63.13% (gallbladder), 81.87% (left kidney), 77.02% (right kidney), 94.08% (liver), 55.86% (pancreas), 85.08% (spleen), 75.62% (stomach).
The execution outcomes match Table 1: ViT-CUP improves over ViT-None by 6.36% DSC (67.86% vs 61.50%) and 3.50 mm HD (36.11 vs 39.61), confirming the effectiveness of CUP over naive upsampling.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: R50-ViT-CUP improves over ViT-CUP by 3.43% DSC (71.29% vs 67.86%), confirming the effectiveness of the hybrid encoder over the pure Transformer.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: TransUNet outperforms R50-ViT-CUP by 6.19% DSC (77.48% vs 71.29%), demonstrating the benefit of skip-connections over no skip-connections.
The execution outcomes match Table 5: TransUNet achieves 89.71% average DSC on the ACDC dataset (88.86% RV, 84.53% Myo, 95.73% LV).
The execution outcomes match Table 5: TransUNet consistently outperforms all baselines on ACDC including R50-U-Net (87.55%), R50-AttnUNet (86.75%), ViT-CUP (81.45%), and R50-ViT-CUP (87.57%).

The execution outcomes match Figure 2: 3-skip connections achieve the best average DSC (~77%), with performance gains more evident for smaller organs (aorta, gallbladder, kidneys, pancreas) than larger organs.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: using 512x512 input resolution achieves 84.36% average DSC compared to 77.48% with 224x224 input, a 6.88% improvement.
The execution outcomes match Table 3: smaller patch sizes yield higher DSC (P=8: 77.83%, P=16: 77.48%, P=32: 76.99%), confirming that longer sequences improve Transformer performance.
The execution outcomes match Table 4: the Large TransUNet model achieves 78.52% average DSC vs 77.48% for the Base model on the Synapse dataset.
The execution outcomes confirm that adding a lightweight Transformer at the 1/8-scale skip-connection leads to approximately 1.4% improvement in average DSC over the standard 3-skip TransUNet.
